title: Setup wizard hangs at main page on fresh Pi image; cannot click setup type or Next
description: After flashing latest main to a fresh Pi image, the setup wizard loads but is unresponsive: clicking the deployment-mode cards or the Next button does nothing. Likely a JS error during page init (recently added on-load code: PI_GROCY_DEFAULT injection, loadHardwareDetect, AP status fetch) that aborts before click handlers wire up. Investigate and fix.
close_reason: Fixed: PI_GROCY_DEFAULT was built as a raw quoted Jinja string that autoescaping mangled to ", breaking the whole inline script (selectInstallMode/wizNext undefined). Routed through tojson filter. Verified rendered JS parses clean with node --check under autoescape.

title: Pi remote: setup wizard re-appears after reboot, settings not persisted
description: A pi_remote device that was successfully configured (server URL + API key entered) falls back to the setup wizard after a reboot. is_configured() returns False because remote_server_url and/or upstream_api_key are not present in settings.json. Root causes to investigate: (1) settings.save() not called after the pi_remote wizard completes; (2) DATA_DIR environment variable not set in the systemd unit, so settings.json is written to a temp path; (3) the satellite config pull overwrites settings.json before is_configured() can read the pi_remote fields. Fix: ensure wizard save includes deployment_mode + remote_server_url + upstream_api_key, and that DATA_DIR is correctly set in the venv service unit.
close_reason: Completed the fix by allowing empty/blank environment variables to be overridden by settings.json during the overlay load.

title: First-run pi_hosted: kiosk service, Stream Deck service, and other installs fail on fresh image
description: On a fresh pi_hosted image first boot, multiple services fail to install: kiosk (Chromium autostart) fails, Stream Deck service pip install fails, and possibly others. Root causes to investigate: (1) Python venv not yet created when service installer runs; (2) apt packages not yet installed (chromium-browser, python3-dev, libhidapi-libusb0); (3) systemd unit files reference paths that do not exist yet; (4) install order in firstboot.sh may be wrong. Fix: audit firstboot.sh install order, add pre-flight dependency checks, and capture install failures to a log file readable from the setup UI.
close_reason: Completed the fix by ensuring apt-get update is run before targeted installs, adding retry loops, checking python3-venv health, and fixing test compatibility.

title: WiFi fallback AP mode: if Pi cannot connect to WiFi, create hotspot and redirect to captive portal WiFi setup
description: On first boot (or when saved WiFi credentials fail) the Pi should fall into a fallback hotspot mode instead of hanging. Requirements: (1) firstboot.sh detects WiFi connect failure after N seconds; (2) if failed, configure hostapd + dnsmasq to create an AP with SSID FoodAssistant-Setup and a fixed password; (3) all DNS from connected clients resolves to the Pi so any URL opens the captive portal; (4) captive portal page shows WiFi SSID scanner and credential entry form; (5) on submit, write wpa_supplicant config and restart networking; (6) on success, disable AP mode and reboot into normal mode. Captive portal should work on iOS, Android, and desktop browsers.
close_reason: Wi-Fi fallback AP mode shipped: configure_wifi_ap_fallback in firstboot.sh installs hostapd/dnsmasq and an ap-watchdog unit that starts a hotspot when wlan0 fails to associate; host bridge /ap/status and /ap/disable plus a setup-page banner drive the captive setup flow.

title: Mealie 3.19+: recipe import PATCH 500s (instructions need ingredientReferences)

title: Pi Host: display rotation leaks to all web browsers, not just the kiosk display
description: Setting the kiosk HDMI display rotation also rotates the web UI shown in a regular phone/PC browser. Rotation must apply ONLY to the attached kiosk display (kiosk mode), never to normal browser sessions.
close_reason: kiosk-auto.js now gates auto-enable on a loopback hostname (v0.6.97), so only the appliance's own display (loaded over localhost by the kiosk service) enters kiosk mode; remote phone/laptop browsers no longer inherit the display rotation/scale. Note: a browser that already latched kioskMode stays rotated until kiosk mode is toggled off (nav tablet icon) or site data is cleared.

title: Pi Host: Grocy browser link shows http://grocy/ (docker service name), unreachable from phone/PC

Browser-facing Grocy links must rewrite docker service names (grocy/mealie/ollama) to the LAN host:port, like localhost is rewritten. API base_url wiring is unaffected.

title: HA cameras unreachable: LLAT in query rejected; proxy via app + bearer on deck
description: Cameras discovered from Home Assistant embed the long-lived access token as ?token= in camera_proxy/camera_proxy_stream URLs, but HA rejects the LLAT in the query string (it needs Authorization: Bearer), so the browser img/video gets 401 and shows 'Camera unavailable'. Fix: store the HA entity_id on the camera; the app proxies HA cameras server-side with the bearer header for the kiosk page, and the deck fetches HA directly with the bearer header (it can set headers). Auto-recover existing token-baked entries by parsing the entity from the stored URL.
close_reason: HA cameras bound to entity + fetched with bearer header: app proxy for kiosk page, deck fetches HA directly; legacy token-baked URLs auto-recovered. Tests added (775 pass).

Each satellite had its own SQLite pending table, so a scan on a Pi Remote was invisible on the server and other satellites. Fix: on a satellite, all /pending endpoints (scan, list, count, patch, delete, commit) forward to the main server using the upstream API key, like the Grocy/Mealie proxy. The server owns the single pending list; satellites are thin UI/scanner front-ends.

title: Barcode scans on Add Food page not persisted, not visible on server
description: Two bugs: (1) Add Food page queue was JS in-memory only; lookupBarcode called analyze/barcode and pushed to queue[], which evaporated on page leave and never appeared on the server. (2) Items scanned on a Pi Remote were never visible on the server because each instance has its own SQLite DB. Fixed by routing lookupBarcode through POST /pending/scan, persisting immediately to local SQLite. Satellite scans are reviewed on the satellite Pending page and committed to shared Grocy. Committed 2f8c66d.
close_reason: Fixed in 2f8c66d — lookupBarcode now POSTs to /pending/scan instead of adding to in-memory queue.

title: Pi display UI: nav overlaps content in landscape, poor touch usability, API link strands kiosk
description:
On the attached Pi touchscreen (Hosyond 800x480, landscape) the navbar has three problems:

1. OVERLAP: navbar uses navbar-expand-md (expands >=768px). At 800px the full horizontal menu shows but the 10+ tabs plus API plus right-side icons do not fit, so the bar wraps to a second row. body has a fixed padding-top:60px assuming a single row, so the wrapped second row overlaps page content.

2. TOUCH: cramped horizontal nav links are hard to hit on a capacitive touchscreen. kiosk.css bumps padding but the horizontal layout is still tight.

3. API LINK STRANDS KIOSK: the API (Swagger /docs) link uses target=_blank, opening a new tab. On the cage/chromium kiosk there is no tab UI, so the user cannot get back. It also cannot be hidden (it lives outside the nav_order/nav_hidden customization system).

Fix direction: raise the collapse breakpoint so the small landscape panel collapses to a hamburger drawer with large vertical tap targets (no wrap, no overlap); hide the API link (and other keyboard/dev-only controls) in kiosk mode; verify on the 800x480 Hosyond panel.
close_reason: Nav collapse breakpoint raised to lg, kiosk-hide class hides API/keyboard links, collapsed menu gets max-height+scroll. Committed e35ea73.

title: pi_remote kiosk: cage fails DRM session (no seat0 logind session); install seatd in firstboot
description:
On a freshly provisioned Pi4 remote, foodassistant-kiosk.service (cage) crash-loops with: libseat seatd/seat.c 'Could not open tty0 ... Permission denied', backend 'Timeout waiting session to become active', 'Failed to start a DRM session', 'Unable to create the wlroots backend'. Root cause: the kiosk user has no seat0 logind session. loginctl shows only seatless sessions (SSH + linger manager, SEAT=-). The unit's PAMName=login + TTYPath=/dev/tty1 path is not producing an active seat0 session on this image, so libseat falls back to its root-only builtin backend and cage (running as fass) is denied VT/DRM access. The display itself is fine (DSI-1 connected, 800x480, backlight on).

Fix verified on-device: install the seatd daemon, enable it, add the kiosk user to the _seatd group, and set LIBSEAT_BACKEND=seatd for the unit (via /etc/foodassistant/kiosk-env). seatd runs as root and brokers DRM/VT to the user without needing an active logind seat session.

Action: in firstboot.sh install_kiosk (and the Pi Hosted kiosk path), apt-get install seatd, systemctl enable --now seatd, usermod -aG _seatd <kuser>, and write LIBSEAT_BACKEND=seatd into the kiosk env file (or as an Environment= line in the unit). Keep this idempotent. Consider whether logind seat attachment can be fixed instead, but seatd is the simplest reliable path for a headless-provisioned cage kiosk.
close_reason: install_kiosk now installs+enables seatd, adds the kiosk user to _seatd, and sets LIBSEAT_BACKEND=seatd on the unit (v0.6.92). Idempotent; covers Pi Remote and Pi Hosted (both re-run firstboot install_kiosk). Verify on-device that foodassistant-kiosk.service no longer crash-loops and cage gets a DRM session.

title: pi_remote: kiosk freeze/no-launch after touch calibration + first-dot calibration failure after rotation recovery
description:
Three linked symptoms observed on pi3remote after the j11 rotation + 8ji web-calibration work landed:

1) Freeze after calibration: completing the four-corner touch calibration wizard leaves the unit frozen showing 'a code window' (looks like a TTY/console, not the kiosk). Suspect the host bridge's systemctl restart of foodassistant-kiosk.service while the calibration page is still being served, or ExecStartPost running foodassistant-apply-rotation before the cage Wayland socket is ready, leaving cage half-up.

2) Cloud-init target on reboot: after a reboot the unit only reaches the cloud-init target and never brings up the kiosk/app. Kiosk service appears not to start (or starts and exits). Need to capture 'systemctl status foodassistant-kiosk' and the journal for the unit at boot.

3) First-dot calibration failure after recovery: once the unit is recovered by changing rotation from the web UI, re-running touch calibration fails at the very first crosshair (first tap not registered or mis-handled). May be related to the evtest SSE stream, the input-group permission, or the rotation/preTransform interaction.

Repro context: cage 0.2.0 / wlroots 0.18.2, DRM backend, connector HDMI-A-2. Rotation now applied via wlr-randr (foodassistant-apply-rotation) on kiosk ExecStartPost. Touch calibration applied via host bridge POST /touch/calibrate which restarts the kiosk.

Next steps: get journalctl -u foodassistant-kiosk.service -b output from a failed boot; check whether ExecStartPost failure (exit nonzero) is taking the unit down; consider making apply-rotation non-fatal and decoupling kiosk restart from the calibration HTTP response.
notes: Re-audit (2026-06): the touch calibration page was crashing (deprecated TemplateResponse signature) and is fixed in 81fcb19. Re-test the post-calibration kiosk freeze/no-launch on-device now that the calibration page loads.

title: Finish ADS7846 touch calibration test on pi3remote and bake into firstboot
description: RESUME. Touch hardware works (ads7846 bound, irq 185, event1). Calibration matrix computed from 4-corner evtest and written to /etc/libinput/local-overrides.quirks on foodassistant-pi3remote; kiosk restarted (active). NOT YET CONFIRMED taps land correctly. Corners (raw, post swapxy=1): TL=(385,595) TR=(371,3460) BL=(3588,410) BR=(3650,3500). Axes crossed: screen-horizontal=raw Y, screen-vertical=raw X. AttrCalibrationMatrix=0 1.3753 -0.1688 1.2635 0 -0.1166 (validated to ~1%). NEXT: (1) apt-get install -y libinput-tools then libinput quirks list /dev/input/event1 to confirm matrix picked up; (2) tap-test kiosk UI; (3) bake tuned default + calibration helper into firstboot configure_touch / setup UI; (4) add libinput-tools to configure_touch deps.
notes: BAKE-IN DONE (commit 40479a5): ADS7846_DEFAULT_MATRIX=0 1.3753 -0.1688 1.2635 0 -0.1166 is now the firstboot default for ads7846 at rotation 0 (explicit TOUCH_CALIBRATION_MATRIX still wins; rotated panels fall back to the rotation matrix). configure_touch installs libinput-tools + evtest and ships /usr/local/bin/foodassistant-touch-calibrate (4-corner tap capture + least-squares affine fit). Tests cover matrix priority and the solver (417 passing). REMAINING (needs pi3remote hardware): (1) apt is already handled by firstboot; run 'libinput quirks list /dev/input/event1' to confirm the matrix is picked up; (2) tap-test the kiosk UI; (3) if off, run foodassistant-touch-calibrate to re-tune.
close_reason: Touch calibration working: fixed read_minmax pressure-axis bug, added web-UI 4-corner calibration that runs on the Pi kiosk display, apply via host bridge (root), and kiosk restart so cage reloads the matrix. Confirmed accurate on pi3remote.

title: Pi remote: failed pip install leaves foodassistant-remote crash-looping 203/EXEC
description: On pi_remote, deploy_remote_service ran pip install of service/requirements.txt with a single attempt and only a warn on failure. When the install failed (transient network or mirror issue), uvicorn was never placed in the venv, so the systemd unit crash-looped forever on status=203/EXEC (Unable to locate executable .../venv/bin/uvicorn) and port 80 refused all connections. Hardened firstboot to retry the pip install with backoff and to verify the uvicorn binary exists, warning loudly with the exact recovery command when it does not. Live recovery: run venv/bin/pip install -r service/requirements.txt then restart the unit.
close_reason: Closed

title: Stream Deck service crash-loops: package copied one level too deep
description: firstboot configure_streamdeck did 'mkdir -p $sd_dst; cp -a $sd_src $sd_dst/', nesting the package at /opt/foodassistant/foodassistant_streamdeck/foodassistant_streamdeck/. The outer dir has no __main__.py, so 'python -m foodassistant_streamdeck' fails with 'package cannot be directly executed' and the service crash-loops despite hardware being detected. Fixed by rm -rf target then cp -a src dst (no trailing slash).
close_reason: Closed

Added _http_serving probe + tests. REMAINING (wave 2, needs setup.html which a subagent is editing): make the frontend startMealie poll /setup/mealie/status until state==running before telling the user to open Mealie, and surface an Open Mealie link only when serving.

Likely ordering/permission/timing during provisioning. Collect journalctl for foodassistant-kiosk.service and foodassistant-streamdeck.service.
close_reason: Fixed: streamdeck package copy path (no longer nested), udev trigger after rule install, separate cage/chromium installs with absolute binary paths. Kiosk confirmed working on pi_host.

title: pi_remote reverts to setup wizard after reboot (empty REMOTE_SERVER_URL env shadows settings.json)
description: On a pi_remote venv install, the systemd remote.env wrote REMOTE_SERVER_URL= (empty) when the URL was entered later via the web wizard rather than at install time. An empty env var still counts as set to pydantic, shadowing the saved settings.json value, so is_configured() returns False and the device shows the setup wizard on every reboot. Fix: firstboot only writes REMOTE_SERVER_URL when non-empty; config.py self-heals the satellite link fields (remote_server_url, upstream_api_key) from settings.json when the live value is blank.
close_reason: Fixed: firstboot omits empty REMOTE_SERVER_URL; config self-heals satellite link fields from settings.json; ui_theme added to SATELLITE_PULL_FIELDS.

title: Mealie starts but is not accessible: port/network issue on pi_hosted
description: After docker compose up Mealie reports started but the UI at http://<hostname>:3000 is not reachable. Possible causes: (1) Mealie container is binding to 127.0.0.1 instead of 0.0.0.0 — check docker-compose port mapping is \3000:9000\ not \127.0.0.1:3000:9000\; (2) ufw or iptables firewall blocking port 3000; (3) Mealie taking longer than expected to initialize — the health check passes the container started check but the app is not yet serving; (4) docker network bridge not routing correctly on Pi OS. Add a post-start health check to the setup wizard that polls http://localhost:3000/api/app/about with retries and shows a clear error if Mealie never becomes reachable.
close_reason: Mealie readiness fixed: host bridge probes HTTP port 9285 (_http_serving) and reports running only when it actually serves; startMealie() in the setup page polls status until running before showing the Open Mealie link.

title: Pi remote touchscreen not working: Waveshare HDMI display Pi Hat
description: The Waveshare HDMI touchscreen Pi Hat is not recognized as a touch input device on pi_remote. The display works (HDMI output fine) but touch events are not registered. Waveshare displays typically need: (1) a custom dtoverlay in /boot/config.txt (e.g. dtoverlay=waveshare-hdmi-touch); (2) libinput or evdev rules for the USB HID touch controller; (3) Chromium launched with --touch-events=enabled and possibly --use-gl=egl. Add Waveshare touchscreen as a setup wizard option in the display configuration pane. firstboot.sh should apply the correct dtoverlay and udev rules when this display type is selected.
close_reason: Closed

title: Setup wizard: live install progress console window for services (Mealie, Stream Deck, kiosk etc)
description: When pi_hosted setup triggers a service install (Mealie via Docker, Stream Deck service, kiosk/Chromium service) the UI shows no feedback — the user sees a spinner and has no idea if installation is succeeding or stalled. Implement a server-sent events (SSE) endpoint that streams stdout/stderr from the install subprocess in real-time. The setup page should open a scrollable terminal-style console pane that auto-scrolls to the bottom and shows exit status (success/fail) when the process ends. Attach to: Mealie docker pull/up, pip install for streamdeck, kiosk service setup.
close_reason: Live install progress console shipped: host bridge _tail_log + /logs/<name> endpoint, with per-action install-log panels in the setup page.

title: Mealie URL: replace local IP with mDNS hostname to survive IP changes
description: Mealie is configured with the Pi LAN IP (e.g. http://192.168.1.x:3000) both internally and in public links. This breaks whenever the Pi gets a new DHCP address. Replace the default mealie_base_url and mealie_public_url with the mDNS hostname: http://<hostname>.local:3000. In pi_hosted mode, firstboot.sh should write the mDNS hostname into settings.json defaults so the user never sees a bare IP. Same fix applies to Grocy.
close_reason: Pre-populated Mealie public URL with mDNS local hostname in seed_app_settings on first boot, and suggested it by default in the setup wizard on Pi.

title: Grocy URL: replace localhost/127.0.0.1 with device hostname in setup defaults and links
description: Grocy is configured with http://127.0.0.1:9383 (docker internal) for API calls, but the setup UI shows this address as the browser-facing link. Clicking it fails because 127.0.0.1 from the users browser points to their laptop, not the Pi. Fix: (1) grocy_public_url should default to http://<hostname>.local:9383 (or device LAN IP) not 127.0.0.1; (2) the test-connection button returns 401 on 127.0.0.1:9383 because the Grocy API key is required — ensure test sends the key; (3) document that grocy_base_url stays as the docker-internal address for API calls but grocy_public_url must be the LAN address for browser links. Using a static IP is also fragile — recommend mDNS hostname (.local) as the default.
close_reason: Pre-populated Grocy public URL with mDNS local hostname in seed_app_settings on first boot, suggested it in the wizard on Pi, and verified API key testing works.

title: docker-compose.remote.yml: port 80 conflicts with venv path
description: docker-compose.remote.yml maps 80:9284, but firstboot.sh for pi_remote also binds the venv service directly on port 80 (via iptables PREROUTING). If both are running simultaneously they conflict. Document clearly that docker-compose.remote.yml is an ALTERNATIVE to the venv path, not an addition. Add a guard in firstboot.sh or a README note: if DEPLOYMENT_MODE=pi_remote and Docker is present, decide which path to use. Consider disabling the venv service if the Docker compose is running.
close_reason: Added remote_docker_stack_active guard in firstboot.sh so the venv service is skipped when the docker-compose.remote.yml stack already holds port 80; documented the two paths as mutually exclusive in the compose header.

title: Tests: satellite integration tests (happy path + unreachable server)
description: tests/test_satellite.py covers unit-level _apply_config and _apply_defaults. Missing: integration tests for sync_from_upstream() against a mock HTTP server. Need: (1) happy path: mock returns valid config payload, assert fields applied and providers invalidated; (2) unreachable server: assert returns ok=False, existing config untouched; (3) bad API key: assert 401 handled gracefully; (4) partial payload (missing expiry_defaults): assert config applied, defaults skipped.
close_reason: Closed

title: pi_remote venv: OTA update mechanism
description: Pi Remote devices run the FoodAssistant service in a Python venv (no Docker). There is currently no way to update the software on these devices — no image pull, no git pull hook. Options: (a) simple cron git pull + pip install + service restart; (b) systemd path unit watching a version file; (c) in-app update button that calls a host bridge script. Pick one and implement. Install script should pre-wire the chosen mechanism at firstboot.
close_reason: Closed

title: Satellite setup UI: sync status panel and manual retry
description: The pi_remote setup pane shows read-only mirrored settings but gives no feedback about sync health. Add a Sync Status panel showing: last sync timestamp, which fields were applied, and whether the last pull succeeded. Include a Sync Now button that hits /setup/satellite/sync and shows the result inline. This is critical UX for debugging a satellite that cannot reach its server.
close_reason: Closed

title: Satellite: periodic config sync (startup + cron)
description: Currently sync_from_upstream() runs once at app startup (lifespan). A satellite that runs for days will drift from the main server as keys/settings change. Add a background periodic pull: recommend APScheduler or asyncio background task every N minutes (configurable, default 15min). Startup pull should be best-effort and non-blocking; periodic pull should log warnings but never crash the app.
close_reason: Satellite performs periodic background config re-sync (startup plus interval, satellite_sync_minutes).

title: arch/modular: three-mode deployment architecture
description: Tracks all work to land the modular deployment architecture from the arch/modular branch into main. The branch introduces three first-class deployment modes (server, pi_hosted, pi_remote) with feature flags, a satellite config-pull protocol, and a no-Docker pi_remote venv path. This epic covers the remaining gaps before the branch is ready to merge.
close_reason: Three-mode deployment architecture (server, pi_hosted, pi_remote) implemented and merged into main.

title: Satellite config pull: fetch keys/defaults from main server
description: Satellite holds only the server URL + an API key. On startup and on demand it GETs a protected config endpoint on the main server and pulls AI keys/models, Grocy URL+key, Mealie URL+key, recipe keys, staples, and expiry defaults. It then talks directly to Grocy/Mealie/AI. Pulled values (esp. defaults) are READ-ONLY on the satellite. Server exposes GET /api/config/satellite, auth via X-API-Key == settings.api_key.
close_reason: Satellite config-federation implemented: server config endpoint, satellite sync, features()/is_configured updates, firstboot satellite wiring

title: firstboot: pi_remote installs app only, no local Grocy/Mealie
description: pi_remote firstboot deploys the full FoodAssistant app (venv on port 80) but does not install Docker/Grocy/Mealie. Drop the proxy REMOTE_SERVER_URL env; the wizard collects the server's Grocy/Mealie/AI-upstream URLs. Keep DATA_DIR + ownership fixes.
close_reason: Satellite config-federation implemented: server config endpoint, satellite sync, features()/is_configured updates, firstboot satellite wiring

title: Pivot pi_remote to full-app + shared backends
description: Redefine pi_remote: the satellite runs the full FoodAssistant app (full UI/features) but installs no local Grocy/Mealie Docker stack. It points grocy_base_url/mealie_base_url at the server's LAN addresses and centralizes AI to the server. Removes the proxy/relay model (REMOTE_SERVER_URL forwarding, feature-flag UI gutting, pane-remote). Grocy remains the inventory source of truth; multiple front-ends share it.
notes: Periodic background re-sync landed (satellite_sync_minutes). Remaining: read-only rendering of server-sourced backend panes on a satellite (overlaps 1cq).
close_reason: Pivot complete: full-app satellite with config sync, server-side Grocy/Mealie proxy, periodic re-sync, and PIN lock. Backend panes are hidden on a satellite (managed via the Main Server pane + sync) rather than shown read-only. The broader LAN device-management platform is tracked separately in 1cq.

title: setup wizard: pi_remote uses full flow, not proxy flow
description: Revert the pi_remote special-casing in setup.html: restore Inventory/AI/Recipes sidebar panes and the full wizard step sequence for pi_remote (keep Hardware). A 'server address' entry can pre-fill Grocy/Mealie/AI-upstream. Remove pane-remote and the REMOTE_SERVER_URL-only path.
close_reason: Wizard + settings sidebar reworked for satellite: Main Server pane (URL+API key+Sync), full local app flow

title: config: redefine features()/is_configured for satellite mode
description: features() should stop hiding Inventory/AI/Recipes config panes for pi_remote (you still configure where remote Grocy/Mealie live). Gate only local-Docker-stack management controls behind a manages_stack flag. is_configured(pi_remote) should require Grocy like the full app, not remote_server_url.
close_reason: Satellite config-federation implemented: server config endpoint, satellite sync, features()/is_configured updates, firstboot satellite wiring

title: Setup wizard: pi_remote flow
description: The setup wizard has no dedicated flow for pi_remote mode. A pi_remote only needs: (1) select remote mode, (2) enter the server URL, (3) optionally configure kiosk display and Stream Deck pointing at that server. The wizard should skip all Docker/Grocy/Mealie steps for remote mode and validate connectivity to the remote before completing.
close_reason: Settings sidebar gates local_stack panes behind features flag; pane-remote added for pi_remote URL config

title: Pi remote: server URL set via web wizard, not SSH
description: Currently setting up a pi_remote requires SSHing in to set REMOTE_SERVER_URL. The server URL should instead be configured through the setup wizard web interface, which is reachable on the Pi's IP even before a remote URL is set. The SSH/config.env path should remain as a fallback but the primary UX must be the browser.
close_reason: Remote server URL now configured via pane-remote in setup.html; firstboot.sh no longer requires REMOTE_SERVER_URL in config.env

title: Pi server: serve web UI on port 80, robust .local DNS
description: On a pi_hosted appliance install, the FoodAssistant web interface should be reachable on port 80 (so users can browse to foodassistant.local without specifying a port). This must be Pi-only to avoid conflicting with existing web servers on a general Linux host. Also audit .local mDNS/Bonjour addressing to ensure it works across Linux, macOS, Windows, and iOS clients on the same LAN.
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix: 'Start Mealie on this device' times out
description: The web button 'Start Mealie on this device' returns 'Timed out starting Mealie.' The host bridge _mealie_start runs 'docker compose --profile with-mealie up -d' with a 120s timeout; the first run pulls the Mealie image, which exceeds 120s on a Pi, so the bridge returns 500 while the pull keeps going. Fix by running the pull/up in the background and polling a status endpoint (mirror the kiosk/streamdeck install pattern), or pull the image first with a longer budget. Verify the compose context (REPO_DIR) and the with-mealie profile resolve on the appliance.
close_reason: Closed

title: On-device SSH installer (curl|bash loader): pick mode + add-ons, then provision
description: Replace the PC-side boot-partition payload flow (edit config.env, run prepare-image.ps1/.sh --boot-dir) with an on-device interactive loader. User flow: 1) Raspberry Pi Imager flashes Pi OS Lite and sets locale/hostname/wifi. 2) Boot the Pi. 3) SSH in and run a one-liner curl|bash that fetches the loader from the repo. 4) Loader detects Pi/display/Stream Deck, asks for deployment mode (Pi Hosted / Pi Remote, or Server hosted off-Pi) and add-ons (Mealie, Ollama, kiosk, Stream Deck), then runs firstboot.sh with the right env. Pi Remote installs nothing heavy. No repo files edited and no clone on the user's PC; the on-device clone is done by the loader/firstboot. Retire prepare-image.ps1 and the documented boot-partition flow; keep prepare-image.sh --image for the pre-built turnkey image (CI) as an advanced no-SSH alternative.
notes: Implemented install.sh at repo root: interactive on-device loader. Reads prompts from /dev/tty (works with curl|bash), detects Pi/display/Stream Deck with the same signals firstboot uses, gathers mode + add-ons, then runs firstboot.sh with the chosen env and REPO_DIR=/opt/foodassistant-src. Pi Remote forces Mealie/Ollama off. NONINTERACTIVE=1 + env for unattended; PLAN_ONLY=1 prints a stable plan line. 12 tests in tests/test_installer.py. Deleted prepare-image.ps1; rewrote docs/hardware/sd-image.md and added README Option 3. firstboot.sh, its tests, and the pre-built image CI pipeline are unchanged. Pushed on main (8cdfcb4). Remaining: real on-device run (clone+sudo+provision) cannot be validated from here.
close_reason: On-device SSH installer shipped; boot-partition payload flow retired. Decision logic tested; needs a real Pi run to validate end-to-end provisioning.

title: firstboot 'Already provisioned' done-marker blocks Stream Deck install after a partial run
description: On a fresh stock Pi OS device, the Stream Deck never gets provisioned and the deck stays dark. Sequence: the curl|bash run died early (compose file not found, now fixed) but the done-marker logic / a prior step left /var/lib/foodassistant/firstboot.done in place. Re-running 'sudo bash firstboot.sh' from the checkout now short-circuits with 'Already provisioned (/var/lib/foodassistant/firstboot.done exists); nothing to do. Set FORCE=1 to re-run.' so configure_streamdeck is never reached. Two problems: (1) the done marker is written (or treated as present) even though provisioning did NOT complete all steps -- the marker should only be written after a fully successful run, and a failed/partial run must not mark the device done; (2) provisioning is all-or-nothing: a later step (Stream Deck) cannot run without re-doing everything, and the only escape is FORCE=1 which re-runs the whole script. Consider per-step idempotent markers or step-targeted re-runs so 'install the Stream Deck service' can proceed independently. Immediate workaround for the user: sudo FORCE=1 bash firstboot.sh.
close_reason: Added STEPS= env var to firstboot.sh; done-marker check bypassed, mark_done skipped. User can now run STEPS=streamdeck sudo bash firstboot.sh to provision just the Stream Deck. 3 new tests. 192 total.

title: Deployment mode selection UI (wizard step 1): Server hosted / Pi Hosted / Pi Remote
description: Build the first wizard step as a deployment-mode picker per 92e design. Offer 'Server hosted', 'Pi Hosted', 'Pi Remote'; hide 'Server hosted' when the Pi detection helper reports a Raspberry Pi. Each choice steers the rest of the wizard: Pi Hosted pre-sets local Grocy/Mealie URLs and notes display is auto-detected (works with or without a display, one can be added later); Pi Remote asks only for the remote FoodAssistant server URL and skips heavy install; Server hosted keeps the current manual Grocy/Mealie URL entry. Persist the chosen mode in settings.
close_reason: Mode-select wizard step + server plumbing done: data-driven cards filtered by host, /setup/mode persistence, /setup/test/remote, is_configured() relaxed for pi_remote, wizard skips Grocy/AI for remote. Tests in test_setup_mode.py.

title: Pi Remote mode: thin-client install path (no Docker, no Grocy/Mealie)
description: Implement the Pi Remote deployment mode end to end: the device installs only the Stream Deck controller and/or Chromium kiosk, both pointed at a remote FoodAssistant server URL supplied in the wizard. No Docker, no Grocy, no Mealie, no local FoodAssistant service. Must run on low-spec hardware (Pi 3). Validate the remote URL is reachable before finishing.
close_reason: Pi Remote thin-client install path done independently of the 92e.2 bootstrap-wizard piece: firstboot is_remote_mode() skips Docker+stack, installs only kiosk/Stream Deck pointed at REMOTE_SERVER_URL. 6 DRY_RUN tests pass. (Mode is pre-seeded via config.env/settings.json; on-device no-Docker wizard tracked in 92e.2.)

title: Pi Hosted mode: auto-detect display, fold Stream Deck + kiosk into setup
description: For Pi Hosted mode, the full stack installs (FoodAssistant + Grocy + Mealie) and the wizard pre-sets local service URLs. Kiosk is enabled automatically when a display is detected (has_display) and skipped otherwise; a display added later should light up the kiosk with no reconfiguration. Surface attached-hardware status (display present?, Stream Deck detected?) in the wizard/settings so the user can see and manage them, closing the gap where Stream Deck and display are currently provisioner-only and invisible in the UI.
notes: Hardware status panel implemented: host bridge probes DRM (display) and sysfs USB (Stream Deck vendor 0fd9); /setup/hardware/status proxy; Attached hardware panel in Stream Deck pane. Live verification on Pi hardware still pending.

title: Lightweight first-boot wizard service (defer heavy install until mode chosen)
description: firstboot.sh currently installs Docker + Grocy/Mealie unconditionally on first boot. Change it to stand up only a lightweight setup wizard first (no Docker required), then run the heavy install as a background job ONLY after the user selects a mode that needs it (Pi Hosted). Pi Remote must install nothing heavy and be viable on a Pi 3. This is the core deferral from 92e.
notes:
DONE: install-gating mechanism + settings.json bridge. DEPLOYMENT_MODE drives whether the heavy stack installs; mode is read from config.env or a wizard-written settings.json (settings.json wins). Pi Remote installs nothing heavy.

REMAINING: the true no-Docker bootstrap wizard for choosing the mode ON-DEVICE. Today the web wizard (which picks the mode) runs inside the Docker service, so Pi Hosted installs Docker first and chooses the mode in the running app (fine), but Pi Remote cannot show an on-device wizard before install -- its mode must be pre-seeded via config.env (or settings.json). A lightweight venv-served uvicorn of /setup that runs before Docker, captures the mode, then triggers the matching install, would close this. Needs on-device integration testing (cannot be fully validated via DRY_RUN/CI).

title: Pi hardware detection helper (is this a Raspberry Pi)
description: Add a reusable check for whether the host is a Raspberry Pi by reading /proc/device-tree/model (contains 'Raspberry Pi'). Needed by the setup wizard to decide which deployment modes to offer (hide 'Server hosted' on a Pi) and by firstboot provisioning logic. Expose to both the FastAPI app (Python) and firstboot.sh (bash). Blocks the mode-select UI.
close_reason: Pi detection implemented: app/hardware.py (is_raspberry_pi/board_model, cached, FOODASSISTANT_FORCE_MODEL hook) + firstboot.sh is_raspberry_pi() with FORCE_PI hook. Tests in tests/test_hardware.py (5 passing).

title: Custom appliance image blocks Imager OS customization (no user/SSH/wifi)
description: Raspberry Pi Imager 2.0.7 disables the OS customization tab for custom (non-official) .img.xz images. On Bookworm there is no default pi/raspberry user, so a flashed appliance image boots with no user account, SSH off, and no wifi credentials -- the device is unreachable and unconfigurable without manually dropping ssh/userconf.txt/wpa_supplicant.conf on the boot partition, which defeats the zero-touch goal. Options to evaluate: (a) ship a first-run provisioner that installs onto STOCK Pi OS Lite (which Imager fully customizes) via a documented Imager-customization + boot-script flow, instead of a prebaked image; (b) bake a default user + SSH-enabled + a captive wifi-setup AP into the image so it is reachable out of the box; (c) document the boot-partition file workaround as the official path. Decision gates the v1.5.x appliance distribution story.
close_reason: Documented the Imager limitation in sd-image.md. Stock Pi OS is now the recommended primary path (full Imager customization). Pre-built image section notes the workaround. A bespoke solution (option b from the bead) is a future enhancement.

title: Deferred install: wizard-mode selection before heavy provisioning
description: Currently firstboot.sh installs Docker, builds the full service image, and starts Grocy on every Pi regardless of intended use. A Pi used as a thin remote (Stream Deck + kiosk only, pointing at an existing FoodAssistant server) doesn't need any of that. Heavy install should be deferred until after a setup wizard mode selection step. First boot should only start a lightweight wizard service (small Python/FastAPI app, no Docker). The wizard asks: Full server (install Docker + Grocy + stack) or Remote only (configure Stream Deck URL and kiosk URL). Heavy install runs as a background job only in Full server mode.
design:
Setup wizard step 1 = deployment mode selection. THREE modes (Dan's spec, AIO+Headless collapsed):

1. Server hosted - FoodAssistant installed on a general server; connects to separately running Grocy and/or Mealie on that same server. NOT offered when we detect the device is a Raspberry Pi.
2. Pi Hosted - everything runs on the Pi (FoodAssistant + Grocy + Mealie). WORKS WITH OR WITHOUT A DISPLAY: kiosk is enabled automatically when a display is detected (has_display), skipped otherwise. A display can be added later and will light up the kiosk with no reconfiguration. Because everything is local, the wizard pre-sets service locations (Grocy/Mealie URLs) automatically instead of asking. This single mode replaces the earlier separate 'AIO Pi Hosted' (with display) and 'Pi Headless' (no display) -- display presence is auto-detected, not a user choice.
3. Pi Remote - thin client. Connects to an existing FoodAssistant server elsewhere on the LAN; this device is only a remote control surface (Stream Deck and/or kiosk pointed at the remote server). Runs on low-spec hardware (Pi 3): NO Docker, NO Grocy/Mealie install. Only asks for the remote server URL.

Detection: wizard must know whether it is running on a Pi (check /proc/device-tree/model for 'Raspberry Pi') to hide 'Server hosted' and offer the two Pi modes.

Install gating: heavy install (Docker + Grocy/Mealie) only happens for Pi Hosted and Server hosted. Pi Remote installs nothing heavy. Mode selection MUST come before provisioning; first boot stands up only a lightweight wizard, not the full stack.
notes: Implementation broken into sub-task beads. Scheduled for fresh implementation at 2:30 AM 2026-06-19.

title: Default LLM model gemini-1.5-flash is unavailable
description: The setup wizard / config default vision+enrichment model 'gemini-1.5-flash' is no longer available from Google and fails the connection test. User confirmed 'gemini-2.5-flash' works. Update defaults to a currently-available model, and ideally fetch the live model list per provider so defaults stay valid. Also surface a link to Google's API key + model docs in the wizard.
close_reason: Default model updated to gemini-2.5-flash across config, wizard, and provider. Live model-list fetching remains a nice-to-have under the wizard redesign (0zz).

title: Bring up HDMI kiosk on the appliance (cage + Chromium)
description: Enable and verify full-screen Chromium kiosk on the Pi's HDMI display showing the FoodAssistant UI. configure_kiosk in firstboot.sh already installs cage+chromium and writes a service; get it running on the test unit, confirm it survives reboot, and integrate UI scaling + (later) the Stream Deck CDP nav (ckd).

title: Scalable UI for small and large displays (UI scale setting)
description: The web UI must look good on a tiny HDMI panel and on a large monitor. Add a UI scale setting (e.g. Small/Medium/Large or a numeric zoom) selectable in Settings and the setup wizard, applied app-wide (Chromium kiosk supports CSS zoom; rem-based scaling for browsers). Goal: legible, well-proportioned layout across display sizes without manual CSS edits. Immediate need for the Raspberry Pi HDMI kiosk test.
close_reason: UI scale setting (Small/Normal/Large/Extra large) implemented as a document zoom, selectable in Settings>Interface with immediate apply, stored server-side. Per-display-size auto-mapping in the wizard is tracked under 0zz.

title: Redesign setup wizard as a guided, step-by-step flow
description: The current /setup page is a dense single form; users cannot tell what is required vs optional and get stuck (e.g. the hidden requirement to set a login password silently blocks completion). Replace with a guided landing experience that walks through steps (welcome, Grocy, AI provider, security/password, optional integrations, done) with clear required-field indicators, inline validation, and an explicit Finish that lands on the app. Surfaced repeatedly during hardware test.
design: Guided multi-step wizard. STEP 1 Choose experience (gates the rest): (a) Server-hosted - software install, connect to separately-running Grocy/Mealie on the same server; NOT offered on a Pi. (b) AIO Pi-hosted - everything on the Pi; auto-set internal URLs since all local. (c) Pi Remote - thin client/remote control for an existing FoodAssistant server on the LAN; can run on low-spec (Pi 3). (d) Pi Headless - this device is the FoodAssistant+Grocy+Mealie server, no display. STEP 1a Auth (password + 2FA, strongly recommended) - only for AIO and Pi-headless. STEP 1b Network setup if needed. STEP 2 Display options (AIO or Pi-remote only): Stream Deck present? (6/15/32 keys); HDMI display present? (size S/M/L for scaling, touch?); non-HDMI display present? (size, touch?). STEP 3 Grocy: link to open Grocy in new window, instructions to set password + generate API key, auto-populate Grocy URL when self-hosted. STEP 4 LLM: include a 'no LLM' option (note: limited functionality, can add later, can change provider anytime); default to currently-AVAILABLE models (gemini-1.5-flash is dead, 2.5-flash works); link to Google API key + model docs. STEP 5 Mealie (skippable, do later). STEP 6 External recipes (optional). STEP 7 Remote access (can be 'coming soon'). STEP 8 Barcode scanner (optional): type USB/BT/built-in, BT pairing or USB device select, optional functional test; support all consumer HID scanners and Pi-specific modules. Cross-cutting: must serve software-only self-hosters, turnkey hardware buyers who cannot code/don't understand API keys (core monthly-plan user), and hybrid (buy hardware interface, self-host Grocy/Mealie backends). Appeal to homelab/self-hosted/Home Assistant enthusiasts while remaining approachable for everyone.
notes: Expanded from Dan's detailed spec during the Pi hardware test. Experience-chooser ties into FoodAssistant-crc (client mode) and the display/scaling beads.
close_reason: Closed

title: Make AI optional: device must function without an AI provider
description: is_configured() currently requires a vision provider key, making AI mandatory. The appliance should work without AI (reduced functionality: no photo analysis, no receipt/barcode LLM enrichment, no AI recipe generation) while inventory, expiry tracking, barcode lookup via Open Food Facts, and manual entry all still work. Drop the hard AI requirement from setup, gate AI-only UI affordances behind 'AI configured', and clearly communicate what is disabled.
close_reason: Closed

title: is_configured() rejects the appliance default Grocy URL
description: config.py is_configured() returns False when grocy_base_url equals _DEFAULT_GROCY_URL ('http://grocy:80'). On the self-hosted appliance that default IS the correct internal address, so a correctly-set-up appliance can never complete setup and is stuck in the /setup redirect loop. Workaround during the hardware test: enter the container name 'http://foodassistant-grocy:80' instead. Real fix: distinguish 'unset' from 'equals default' (e.g. track whether the user saved Grocy settings, or drop the default-equality check and validate by a successful connection test instead).
close_reason: Closed

title: Distribute FoodAssistant image without private GHCR auth
description: The appliance compose pulls ghcr.io/syracuse3dprinting/foodassistant:latest, which is private, so a fresh device gets 'unauthorized' and cannot deploy. Options: make the published image public, bake the image into the SD image at build time, or provide a scoped pull token in the provisioner. During the test we worked around it by building from local source on the Pi (slow, requires the repo). Decide and implement before any customer image.
close_reason: Closed

title: prepare-image clobbers Raspberry Pi Imager firstrun.sh (wipes wifi/ssh/user)
description: prepare-image.sh and prepare-image.ps1 both unconditionally write firstrun.sh to the boot partition. Raspberry Pi Imager uses the SAME firstrun.sh + cmdline.txt systemd.run hook to apply wifi credentials, enable SSH, and create the user. Our overwrite destroys all of that, so the device boots with no network and no login (observed: black screen, keyboard dead, not on LAN). Fix: detect an existing firstrun.sh, preserve it (e.g. rename to firstrun-imager.sh) and chain-call it from ours before running our provisioner, OR stop using the firstrun.sh filename entirely. This bricked the first boot of the test unit.
close_reason: Closed

title: CRITICAL: appliance imaging is not customer-ready (turnkey epic)
description: Hardware bring-up test showed the current SD-image flow is far too manual for customers or for pre-imaging Pis at scale. Blockers found: (1) FoodAssistant container image is private on GHCR, so a customer device cannot pull it; (2) cloning the repo on Windows injects CRLF that breaks the shell scripts on the Pi; (3) prepare-image overwrites the Raspberry Pi Imager firstrun.sh and wipes wifi/ssh/user; (4) copied files land with root-only 700 perms that break the systemd service; (5) Grocy URL/key not prefilled; (6) Stream Deck install is fully manual. Goal: a turnkey, pre-built image (or one-click installer) a non-technical customer can flash and boot with zero terminal steps. Track sub-issues against this.
notes: Turnkey flash-and-boot achieved: (1) provisioner is config-free with auto-detect of display/Stream Deck and OS timezone, runs services as the real uid-1000 user, kiosk seat/PAM fix baked in; (2) self-healing clone+build fallback when GHCR private; (3) release-image.yml CI builds a ready-to-flash .img.xz attached to GitHub Releases. Remaining: tag a release to produce the first image; optionally make GHCR package public for fast first boot.
close_reason: All code-side blockers resolved. Version bumped to 1.6.0 with full CHANGELOG. Remaining manual step: 'git tag v1.6.0 && git push origin v1.6.0' triggers the release-image.yml CI to build and attach the .img.xz to the GitHub Release. Optionally make the GHCR package public for faster first-boot pulls.

title: Require authentication by default

Middleware fails closed when configured+auth_required but no password. EXCEPTION: requests arriving via HA Ingress are already authenticated by Home Assistant, so bypass app login for them. The HA add-on ships AUTH_REQUIRED=false so users aren't double-prompted.

title: [security] Hash the web-UI password and kiosk PIN at rest (currently plaintext + direct compare)
description:
VERIFIED accurate. ui.py login does secrets.compare_digest(password, settings.auth_password) (ui.py:44-45) and the kiosk PIN likewise (ui.py:78), so auth_password and kiosk_pin are stored in plaintext (settings.json, chmod 0o600) and compared directly, not hashed/salted. Anyone who reads settings.json (or an AUTH_PASSWORD env var) gets the login password.

Fix: store a salted hash (argon2/bcrypt, or stdlib hashlib.scrypt/pbkdf2 to avoid a new dependency) for auth_password and kiosk_pin; verify by hashing the input. Handle the env-var/plaintext-set path by hashing on first load, and migrate any existing plaintext value on next save. Keep using compare_digest on the hashes (constant-time).

Out of scope / cannot one-way hash: api_key, extra_api_keys, totp_secret, AI provider keys are bearer/shared secrets that must be used as values, so they stay plaintext-at-rest (already 0o600 + redacted from exported backups). Optional at-rest encryption is possible but has limited benefit on a single self-hosted box (the decryption key lives next to the data); track separately if wanted.

Already-correct (from the same audit): secret_key auto-generates via secrets.token_hex(32) on first run (config.py:948-949); compare_digest is constant-time; settings.json is 0o600; SECRET_SETTING_KEYS are redacted from backups.
close_reason: Web-UI password and kiosk PIN are now salted-scrypt hashed at rest: config.save() hashes them before persisting, login and PIN verify via verify_secret, legacy plaintext values still verify and auto-upgrade to a hash on the next successful login. API keys and TOTP secret remain plaintext by necessity (bearer secrets). Tested.

title: Stream Deck editor: multi-page support (author page 2+)
description: The controller already paginates: when more actions are configured than fit, the last key becomes a 'More'/page_next key and the rest continue on the next page (works on the deck). But the web editor is single-page and offers no way to set up the additional pages. Add page navigation to the editor (Page 1/2/..., add/remove page) so users can place keys on every page. The saved flat keys list maps to pages via build_pages (usable = key_count-1 editable per page when paginated; the trailing nav key is auto-inserted, not user-editable).
close_reason: Multi-page editing added (v0.6.120): the grid now has page nav (prev/next, 'Page X of N', Add page) and renders the current page's editable slots mapped to the right indices in the flat keys list, with the reserved auto 'More' key shown but not editable. Pagination math mirrors layout.build_pages exactly (cross-checked), so editor pages match the deck and off-page keys survive a save.

Floating nav remains the single nav surface. Normal scale, phones, desktop, and panels without the floating nav keep the full navbar so all destinations stay reachable. Measured at 1.4x on 800x480: nav chrome dropped from ~46% to ~36% of the effective viewport. All 828 tests pass.

title: Stream Deck: pre-populate default keys into the drag/drop editor
description: The user-configurable drag/drop Stream Deck editor should start pre-populated with the default key set, so users edit from the defaults instead of an empty grid.
notes: REVERTED (commit c85096d) - the first implementation broke the deck. Root cause: the editor is SINGLE-PAGE and saves a flat keys list, but the controller PAGINATES DEFAULT_ORDER (28 actions) across pages for 6/15-key decks and auto-inserts page-nav keys. Pre-filling the grid with a flattened/sliced default (a) showed the wrong first page, (b) ALWAYS saved explicit keys (even unchanged), diverging from the controller's real multi-page default, so 15-key showed nothing usable and 32-key rendered unfilled keys. Correct approach needs either: a paginated editor that mirrors the deck's pages incl nav keys, OR pre-fill from build_pages[0] (true first page) AND save keys:undefined when the grid is unchanged from default (so the controller keeps its full multi-page default), AND strip auto-nav keys on save. Only the 32-key single-page case is simple. Do NOT ship a flat-slice version again.
close_reason: Redone correctly (v0.6.118): the editor now mirrors the deck's RESOLVED config (defaults to full DEFAULT_ORDER, invalid keys dropped) via a new --dump-config that the bridge serves, instead of reading raw config.toml. Fixes the blank 15-key editor (no saved keys) and the stale/mismatched 32-key editor. Also stopped the grid render from truncating off-page keys so editing a small (paginated) deck no longer drops later pages on save.

close_reason: Closed

title: Camera feed on the kiosk display and as a Stream Deck snapshot key, on demand
description: Show a camera feed on demand. (1) Kiosk display: a /ui/camera page that shows a configurable MJPEG/HLS stream full-screen (generic stream URL, or a Home Assistant camera entity via the HA camera_proxy_stream endpoint using the deck/app HA token). A Stream Deck nav key and a UI button open it; support more than one camera with a switcher. (2) Stream Deck: a camera action whose KEY FACE shows a periodically refreshed JPEG snapshot (the deck is a slow USB HID device, so live video on a key is impractical; refresh the snapshot every few seconds while shown). Pressing the key opens the full feed on the kiosk display. Config: a small list of cameras (name, stream_url, snapshot_url, or HA entity). Touches deck config + actions + controller (fetch+set key image) + render helper + ui.py route + camera.html template + app config + setup.html camera config UI. Sequence after the HA-config work (f439) since both touch setup.html and actions.py.
notes: Add a full-deck overlay mode: render the camera snapshot to one canvas sized to the whole key grid and slice it into per-key tiles (skip inter-key gaps), set across all keys; any key press exits back to the normal layout. This is the primary deck representation (a single-key snapshot is the compact alternative). Refresh limited by USB HID throughput (a few fps small decks, ~1fps on a 32-key XL), so it is a periodic snapshot, not live video. Slicing logic must be a pure, tested function. Source: generic stream/snapshot URLs and Home Assistant camera entities (build URLs from ha_base_url + entity + token).
close_reason: App side (oewn): /ui/camera page + config + HA URL helper. Deck side (5l2l): camera snapshot key + full-deck overlay (verified slicing). Both committed.

title: Stream Deck: configure Home Assistant keys (URL/token/slots) and advanced weather options in the UI

title: Settings: add restore-from-backup option
description: Add a UI affordance to restore from a backup archive (scripts/restore.sh exists for CLI). Let the operator upload or select a backup and trigger a restore, with clear warnings. Pairs with the existing Backup & Updates pane.
design:
RESTORE FROM BACKUP — design (FoodAssistant-bkpp)

Two backup scopes exist; the restore design splits along the same line.

SCOPE A (this bead, do now, works on every deployment):
Restore the app-data zip — the exact counterpart to the existing GET /admin/backup.
Covers service/data: settings.json, the SQLite defaults DB, staples.txt. Self-contained:
the app writes its own data dir, no Docker control needed, so it works on server, pi_hosted,
and pi_remote alike.

New endpoint: POST /admin/restore (multipart upload of the zip).
  1. Read the upload into memory; open with zipfile. Reject if not a zip or missing the
     "foodassistant-data/" top-level prefix.
  2. Zip-slip guard: for every member, resolve the target under data_dir and verify it stays
     inside data_dir (no "..", no absolute paths). Skip anything outside.
  3. Snapshot current data first: copy data_dir to data_dir/../data.pre-restore-<stamp> (mirrors
     restore.sh's safety net) so a bad restore is recoverable.
  4. Extract members into data_dir, overwriting.
  5. Secret-preserve-on-blank: if the restored settings.json has empty values for any
     SECRET_SETTING_KEYS (a redacted backup blanks them), keep the currently-stored value for
     that key instead of wiping a working credential. This matches the save() "blank = keep"
     rule and the download note ("after restoring you re-enter keys").
  6. Reload in-memory state: re-read settings.json into settings, engine.dispose() so the new
     SQLite defaults DB is picked up, reset_providers(), reset_mealie/staple caches.
  7. Return {ok, restored_files, snapshot_dir, secrets_preserved}.

UI (Backup pane, beside Download Backup): a file input + "Restore from backup" button with a
confirm dialog ("replaces current settings and database; current data is saved aside first").
Inline result showing files restored and the snapshot path.

Tests: build a zip via the existing _build_zip(), POST it to /admin/restore, assert settings
round-trip; a redacted zip keeps existing secrets; a zip-slip member ("../evil") is rejected.

SCOPE B (out of scope here; separate bead/host path):
Full grocy+mealie snapshot restore needs to stop/start the container stack, so it cannot run
from inside the app container. Pi: route through the host bridge (new POST /restore that runs
scripts/restore.sh as root, like foodassistant-update). Server: host CLI scripts/restore.sh.
Document as the host path; large archives make an in-app upload impractical anyway.

Open question for Dan: also want the Scope-B (Grocy+Mealie) host-bridge restore wired into the
UI for Pi appliances, or is the documented host CLI enough for the full snapshot?
close_reason: Scope A (in-app app-data restore) delivered: POST /admin/restore + Backup pane UI + tests. Full Grocy+Mealie host-snapshot restore tracked separately as FoodAssistant-h18b.

title: In-UI software update for Pi instances (surface and expand the host-bridge OTA)
description: Investigate and build a UI path to update FoodAssistant on Pi instances (pi_hosted and pi_remote) from the web UI. The host bridge already has a POST /update (git pull + venv pip + service restart) used for pi_remote OTA. Determine what is needed for a general, safe in-UI update: version check against the GitHub repo, an Update button with progress/log streaming, handling of pi_hosted (Docker volume-mounted code: git pull applies live, rebuild only for requirements/Dockerfile changes) vs pi_remote (venv) differences, and a clear success/failure/rollback story. design: Review host bridge /update and the existing update checker (admin.py /version, GITHUB_REPO). Decide per deployment mode what 'update' means. Add a UI control (likely in Backup & Updates) with live log streaming like the install panels. Confirm safety (no half-applied state, restart handling).
notes: Implemented in tu0i: foodassistant-update helper now has a Pi Hosted Docker branch (docker compose pull service + up -d --no-deps service, build-from-source fallback), /setup/update gated on is_pi_appliance (pi_hosted OR pi_remote), and the Settings Updates card shows the OTA + auto availability check for both Pi modes. Remaining: verify the Docker path on real Pi Hosted hardware.

title: Enforce semver: every commit bumps at least the patch version
description: Adopt a major.minor.patch discipline where APP_VERSION (service/app/config.py) is the single source of truth and every commit changes at least the patch number. Provide tooling so this is automatic/enforced rather than manual: a bump helper and/or a pre-commit or pre-push hook that increments the patch unless the commit explicitly sets a minor/major bump. Keep CHANGELOG [Unreleased] in sync. Tag releases from APP_VERSION.
design: Options: a scripts/bump-version.sh that edits APP_VERSION and a git hook (.git/hooks or a tracked hook installed by a make target) that auto-increments patch on commit. Decide on conventional-commit detection for minor/major. Must not double-bump on amend/rebase. Surface the version in the UI (already via APP_VERSION).

title: Kiosk/Stream Deck wake: separate timeouts, shared wake activities
description: Wake the kiosk display and/or Stream Deck from their idle-blank state on user activity. Requirements: (1) display and Stream Deck keep SEPARATE idle timeouts (already have streamdeck_idle_timeout and display_idle_timeout settings), (2) ANY wake activity (a Stream Deck button press, a kiosk touch/tap, a keypress, or pointer movement) wakes BOTH surfaces, not just the one that received the input. Today the Stream Deck blanks all keys after streamdeck_idle_timeout and restores on keypress; the kiosk display needs an equivalent blank+wake, and the two need to be cross-wired so activity on either restores both. Relates to fr5 (accelerometer/button wake options).
design: Stream Deck side already blanks/restores in the controller. Add a kiosk display blank (CSS overlay or backlight via host bridge) driven by display_idle_timeout, with wake on touch/click/key/pointermove. Cross-wake: controller key press should poke the app (host bridge or an endpoint) to wake the display, and a kiosk activity should poke the controller to wake the deck. Keep the two timeout values independent.
close_reason: Host bridge owns kiosk display blanking (idle loop + vcgencmd/xset) and brokers activity. Browser kiosk-idle.js and the Stream Deck controller both report activity to the bridge; controller polls it. A screen touch or deck press wakes both surfaces; timeouts stay independent. App proxies activity/blank/wake and pushes display_idle_timeout on save+startup. Tested: bridge pure helpers, controller cross-wake, off-Pi endpoints.

A service restart on the pi_remote cleared it, so the config/health is correct but there is no periodic re-check or cache invalidation for Mealie reachability. Filed from observation against the prior submit; the recent satellite sync-status work (yz6) may have changed behavior here, so confirm against current main before fixing.
close_reason: Confirmed resolved on current main by the satellite sync-status work (yz6), as the bead anticipated. Chain: server /api/config/satellite returns all SATELLITE_PULL_FIELDS including mealie_base_url/api_key/public_url; the satellite re-pulls every satellite_sync_minutes (default 15) via _periodic_satellite_sync; _apply_config applies the fields to live settings; mealie_configured() is a stateless bool(mealie_base_url and mealie_api_key) with no cached unavailable state. So a Mealie started on the host after setup becomes available within one sync interval, no restart. Added regression test test_sync_picks_up_mealie_started_after_setup (mealie_configured False to True across a sync). Full suite 435 green.

description: Make Mealie part of the default pi_hosted install. Also evaluate offering Grocy's own recipe manager as an alternative recipe backend (no extra container). See docs/recipe-backend-comparison.md; scopes a recipe_backend = grocy|mealie setting.
notes:
EVALUATION (Grocy built-in recipe manager as a Mealie alternative):

Findings:
- Mealie default-on for pi_hosted is DONE (firstboot, via bmp).
- The app's entire recipe layer is built on Mealie's API (service/services/mealie.py): search_recipes, get_recipes_with_ingredients, create_recipe_from_url (URL import), meal plan CRUD, shopping list CRUD, plus the inventory matcher (classify_recipes/suggest_recipes). Routers, templates (recipes/cook/mealplan/shopping), and Stream Deck nav keys all assume Mealie.
- Grocy has a native recipe module (recipes tied to products/stock, a meal plan, and Grocy's own shopping list), but service/services/grocy.py implements ZERO recipe support today, and Grocy's recipe UX lacks URL import and rich browsing.

Recommendation: keep Mealie as the primary/default recipe backend. Offer Grocy-native recipes only as an optional lightweight mode for users who want one fewer container, which would require a new recipe-provider abstraction (a GrocyRecipeProvider mirroring the mealie client surface: search/get/mealplan/shopping) plus a settings switch and template adjustments. That is a sizable feature, not a quick toggle.

Decision needed from Dan: ship the Grocy-recipe option (new bead, ~provider abstraction + UI), or keep Mealie-only and close this. The Mealie-default half of this bead is already delivered.

title: Grocy URL pre-fill uses 127.0.0.1, only correct when configuring from the Pi itself

This is correct when a browser runs on the Pi in kiosk mode, but the normal setup flow is a user on a laptop browsing to the Pi by IP. In that case 127.0.0.1 resolves to the user's own machine, not the Pi. The user has to manually change the URL to the Pi's IP (e.g. http://192.168.1.38:9383) to connect, then change it back to http://localhost:9383 for the server's own API calls to work -- or leave it as the IP, which also works. title: Evaluate easiest install path for non-technical / no-homelab users
description: Investigate the lowest-friction ways for users to run FoodAssistant. Options to evaluate: (1) one-line Docker install script, (2) prebuilt published image on GHCR/Docker Hub so users skip the build step, (3) managed/PaaS options for users without their own server (Railway, Fly.io, Render, PikaPods, Elestio), (4) the offline-only mode for fully local. Recommend a primary path and document it. Note the tension: this app needs Grocy too, so any hosted option must account for the multi-container stack.
design:
EVALUATION (2026-06-16):

Core tension: FoodAssistant requires Grocy (+ optional Mealie/Ollama), so single-container PaaS one-click options are a poor fit.

Options scored:
1. One-line install script — low friction, wraps clone+compose. Good SECONDARY (depends on #2).
2. Prebuilt GHCR image + production compose — lowest friction for homelab (Proxmox/TrueNAS/Unraid/Synology). RECOMMENDED for homelab users. Needs: prod Dockerfile stage (no --reload, code baked in, rclone added), GH Actions multi-arch build/push, docker-compose.prod.yml with image: refs.
3. PaaS (Railway/Fly/Render/PikaPods) — poor fit, multi-service + per-service billing + Grocy not a linked add-on. De-prioritize; document a Railway template only as advanced/hosted.
4. Offline-only — already documented in README.

PRIMARY PATH for no-homelab users: Home Assistant ADD-ON.
- HA add-on = Supervisor-managed Docker container. App already ships HA sensors/automations/Lovelace, so audience aligns.
- Ingress embeds UI in HA sidebar, HA handles auth (password/TOTP optional inside HA), no extra port.
- Grocy dependency resolved via existing community Grocy/Mealie HA add-ons + internal Supervisor network.
- Constraints: requires HA OS / HA Supervised (not HA Container/Core — those use compose). Low-power HW => cloud LLM or no-LLM mode.
- TECH GOTCHA (confirmed): Ingress serves under dynamic path prefix. 22 hardcoded absolute paths across 10 templates, no root_path/X-Ingress handling. App must be made Ingress-aware (Starlette root_path + relative template links) before add-on works. This is the bulk of the effort.

RECOMMENDATION: pursue both (A) GHCR image + prod compose for homelab, and (B) HA add-on for the no-homelab HA crowd. They share the production-image work. PaaS documented but not built.
close_reason: All install paths delivered: GHCR image + publish workflow, prod compose + install.sh, Ingress-awareness, and the HA add-on. PaaS skipped per Dan.

title: test_satellite.py sync tests fail when run in isolation (test-ordering dependency)
description: The four test_sync_* tests in tests/test_satellite.py pass in the full suite (python -m pytest tests/) but fail when test_satellite.py is run alone. The failure is an assertion on a URL value (e.g. 'http://g...'), suggesting global settings or provider state is mutated by an earlier test/fixture and not reset before these run standalone. CI runs the full suite so it is green, but the tests are not self-contained. Fix: make the sync tests set up and tear down their own settings state (or add a fixture) so they pass in isolation.
close_reason: Fixed by isolating data_dir to a tmp_path in the satellite_mode fixture. Root cause: settings.save() (invoked via _record_last_sync during sync) calls apply(existing), which re-overlays all _SAVEABLE fields from the real /app/data/settings.json onto the live settings, clobbering the grocy_base_url the pull just applied. The real file held a stale 'http://g', so the test failed standalone but passed in the full suite where an earlier test left the expected value. With per-test data_dir isolation, test_satellite.py now passes alone (12) and in the full suite (434).

title: Resolve google-api-python-client wheel version normalization deprecation (pip 25.3)
description: pip warns that a resolved wheel 'google_api_python_client-1.0beta5prerelease2-py3-none-any.whl' (and '...prerelease') has a non-normalized version; pip 25.3 will hard-error on this 'Invalid wheel filename (invalid version)'. This is pulled in transitively (likely via google-generativeai). Pin google-api-python-client to a current normalized release in service/requirements.txt (or constrain the offending transitive dep) so installs do not break on pip 25.3. See https://github.com/pypa/pip/issues/12938
close_reason: Pinned google-api-python-client==2.197.0 in service/requirements.txt. google-generativeai 0.8.3 depends on it directly, and the index carries the non-normalized 1.0beta5prerelease2 wheel that pip 25.3 hard-errors on. Pin keeps the 1.x/beta line out of the resolution. Verified compatible via pip --dry-run --no-deps.

title: Grocery store API layer for purchase-history import
description: Research and build an integration layer that pulls shopping history from grocery store APIs to auto-populate inventory/purchases. Survey which major grocery chains expose open/partner APIs and loyalty-account data access. Late-stage feature.
design:
API survey (June 2026). No major US grocery chain exposes CONSUMER purchase/order history via an official self-serve API.

TIER 1 - Official, free, but NO purchase history (catalog/cart only):
- Kroger Public API: OAuth, scopes product.compact / cart.basic:write / profile.compact. Can search products, add to cart, get profile id. NO past purchases. Covers Kroger banners (Ralphs, Fred Meyer, King Soopers, Frys, Harris Teeter, Smiths). Useful for the REVERSE direction (push shopping list to Kroger cart) and product enrichment.

TIER 2 - Partner-only (business agreement, gated, weeks of approval):
- Instacart Developer Platform/Connect: partner agreement, 30-40 day approval, registered business required. Build carts/orders/recipes across many retailers; not consumer purchase-history read.
- Walmart APIs: Marketplace/Supplier = SELLER side (orders you receive as a seller), not consumer order history.
- Albertsons Media Collective: advertiser/retail-media, aggregate audiences, not individual history.

TIER 3 - No official API:
- Amazon Fresh / Whole Foods (AWS says no API; SP-API is seller side), Costco, Aldi, Trader Joes, Wegmans, Target consumer. Only third-party scrapers (ToS risk, fragile) or reverse-engineered private APIs (Safeway) - not shippable.

TIER 4 - Store-agnostic ingestion (the real way to cover many stores):
- Receipt OCR from a photo: Tabscanner (free 200/mo) or Veryfi. Works for ANY brick-and-mortar store.
- Email order-confirmation parsing: online grocery orders send itemized emails.
- Manual CSV/history export import: Kroger and Amazon let users download their own purchase history from the website.

RECOMMENDATION: drop the per-chain API framing. Build one store-agnostic purchase-import layer (receipt OCR + emailed-confirmation/CSV import) that maps line items to Grocy products. Optionally add Kroger Public API for the reverse direction (shopping list to cart) and barcode/product enrichment.
notes: Blocked on API selection. Candidates: Kroger API (free, US), Instacart partner program, Walmart Open API, or receipt/CSV import. Need Dan to pick a target store and confirm whether a partner agreement is feasible. No code started.
close_reason: Researched: no major US grocery chain exposes consumer purchase-history via official API (survey in design field). Dan chose store-agnostic receipt OCR, which already ships: Add page receipt radio -> /analyze/receipt -> LLM vision provider parses line items -> import queue -> commit -> Grocy. Works with gemini/openai/anthropic/ollama, any store, no per-chain integration or extra API key. Fixed hardcoded-provider status message. Follow-up x76 filed for capturing receipt purchase date to back-fill historical receipts.
